claim_text: Agent auxiliary produced a class-behavior shift repeatable across five validation seeds relative to the BPB-selected random primary control.
evidence: Agent minus random primary macro-F1 diff 0.0718 [0.0316, 0.1090], F1-no diff 0.2925 [0.2025, 0.3716].
required_wording: Describe this as no-class recovery or a retained-validation class-behavior shift, not as better biomedical reasoning.
forbidden_wording: The agent learned a better biomedical QA model.

claim_text: Agent auxiliary generally outperformed random or manual search.
evidence: Agent minus random macro-F1 best macro-F1 diff 0.0179 [-0.0207, 0.0549]; agent minus manual baseline macro-F1 diff 0.0186 [-0.0187, 0.0563].
required_wording: Say the macro-F1 advantage over the strongest random/manual controls was small and uncertain.
forbidden_wording: Agent outperformed random search.

claim_text: Agent auxiliary improved probabilistic reliability or calibration.
evidence: Agent minus random primary Brier diff 0.0881 [0.0488, 0.1286]; ECE agent 0.1614 vs random primary 0.0321.
required_wording: Say the macro-F1 gain came with worse Brier/ECE calibration diagnostics.
forbidden_wording: Agent auxiliary is better calibrated.

claim_text: Lower language-model validation BPB is a sufficient proxy for PubMedQA downstream behavior.
evidence: Random primary had lower BPB than agent auxiliary (2.1403 vs 2.3770) but lower macro-F1 (0.2573 vs 0.3291).
required_wording: Call BPB an incomplete proxy, especially for macro-F1 and minority-class behavior.
forbidden_wording: BPB predicts biomedical QA ability.

claim_text: A single 5-minute run is sufficient evidence of improvement.
evidence: Repeated validation used five seeds; agent macro-F1 SD was 0.0421, random macro-F1-best SD was 0.0299, and the strongest pairwise CI crossed zero.
required_wording: Call single runs candidate hypotheses until they pass repeated-seed and paired-audit gates.
forbidden_wording: Agent #002 proved improvement by a single run.

claim_text: Frozen BiomedBERT is a stronger macro-F1 reference than the small autoresearch models.
evidence: Frozen BiomedBERT/BiomedBERT macro-F1 was higher than agent auxiliary in the reference audits; the calibration diagnostics varied by reference-run implementation, so this is not a calibration claim.
required_wording: Use as a non-compute-matched biomedical encoder reference line.
forbidden_wording: The small autoresearch model is competitive with PubMedBERT/BiomedBERT.

claim_text: Higher PubMedQA macro-F1 proves abstract-grounded biomedical reasoning.
evidence: Context-use sanity checks showed question-only TF-IDF LR and frozen BiomedBERT were unexpectedly strong.
required_wording: Say PubMedQA behavior requires partial-input and shuffled-context audits.
forbidden_wording: The model reads abstracts in a clinically meaningful way.

claim_text: The selected 60 cases support a supervisor-informed illustrative case-audit taxonomy.
evidence: The integrated 60-case supervisor-informed, standardized table yielded 13 corrected yes-collapse, 12 overcorrected yes, 15 maybe unresolved, 10 both-wrong/ambiguous, eight no-meaningful-change, and two increased-instability cases.
required_wording: Call it a supervisor-informed illustrative audit; do not call it independent blinded consensus.
forbidden_wording: Expert consensus error analysis.

claim_text: SciFact externally validates the autoresearch-trained small causal LM.
evidence: SciFact currently evaluates majority and frozen BiomedBERT reference baselines, not the autoresearch small LM.
required_wording: Call SciFact an external stress test for reference evidence classification behavior.
forbidden_wording: SciFact proves the agent strategy generalizes.

claim_text: The system is clinically useful or ready for decision support.
evidence: No patient-level data, clinical workflow, prospective validation, clinician evaluation, or deployment study was performed.
required_wording: State that this is a biomedical literature QA audit, not clinical decision support.
forbidden_wording: Clinically useful model.

claim_text: The hypothesis-first protocol prospectively governed the reported original search.
evidence: The hypothesis-first protocol was added after the legacy manual/random/agent search and is documented in the retrospective alignment audit.
required_wording: Say the protocol retrospectively grades current evidence and prospectively constrains future runs.
forbidden_wording: The original search was prospectively governed by the hypothesis-first protocol.

claim_text: The manual error audit is an independent expert consensus review.
evidence: The current 60-case analysis is supervisor-informed and standardized, but it was not a blinded independent two-reviewer design with agreement statistics.
required_wording: Call it a supervisor-informed illustrative case audit unless independent reviewer agreement is obtained.
forbidden_wording: Independent expert consensus error analysis.

claim_text: Question+context PubMedQA scores demonstrate abstract-grounded evidence reasoning.
evidence: Context-use sanity checks showed unexpectedly strong question-only TF-IDF and frozen BiomedBERT performance; shuffled-context checks were needed to expose context artifacts.
required_wording: Say context-use checks limit evidence-grounding claims and motivate cautious interpretation.
forbidden_wording: Question+context accuracy proves abstract-grounded biomedical reasoning.

claim_text: Calibration diagnostics are clinical reliability estimates.
evidence: Brier and ECE were computed as validation-set sanity diagnostics on small PubMedQA splits with model-specific probability sources.
required_wording: Call them calibration sanity diagnostics, not clinical reliability estimates.
forbidden_wording: The calibration analysis establishes clinical reliability.

claim_text: The opened PubMedQA test split provides blind held-out confirmation.
evidence: The PubMedQA test split was opened during the audit; later use is restricted to post-freeze audit-only reporting.
required_wording: Call PubMedQA test a post-freeze audit-only split, not an untouched blind test.
forbidden_wording: Held-out blind PubMedQA test confirmation.

claim_text: The fixed 5-minute Apple Silicon budget estimates the best possible low-resource biomedical model.
evidence: The fixed budget is a stress-test constraint for autonomous experimentation and claim safety, not a performance-optimization or scaling study.
required_wording: Call the 5-minute budget a low-resource stress-test budget.
forbidden_wording: Best possible low-resource biomedical QA model.

claim_text: The agent search was blind and independent of earlier manual or random-search results.
evidence: The agent phase occurred after earlier manual/random exploration and is analyzed as hypothesis generation, not a blind benchmark.
required_wording: Say the agent generated a hypothesis after earlier manual/random exploration.
forbidden_wording: Blind independent agent-search benchmark.

claim_text: The study fairly benchmarks agent, random, and manual search algorithms.
evidence: The search spaces and timing were not identical; the repeated audit compares selected configurations and claims.
required_wording: Call this a configuration and claim audit, not an algorithm-level search benchmark.
forbidden_wording: Fair benchmark showing agent-search algorithm superiority.

claim_text: Strong question-only results invalidate PubMedQA.
evidence: Question-only baselines limit abstract-grounding claims but do not invalidate PubMedQA as a stress-test dataset.
required_wording: Say question-only performance motivates context-use guardrails and cautious interpretation.
forbidden_wording: PubMedQA is invalid because question-only baselines are strong.

claim_text: Manual cases prove the PubMedQA labels are correct.
evidence: The manual audit is illustrative and label ambiguity remains, especially for maybe/qualified cases.
required_wording: Say the cases illustrate model behavior and residual label ambiguity.
forbidden_wording: Manual cases prove label truth.

claim_text: Brier/ECE values are directly comparable clinical reliability estimates across all model families.
evidence: Probability sources differ across label-scored LMs, logistic regression, frozen encoders, and degenerate majority baselines.
required_wording: Use Brier/ECE as sanity diagnostics and note probability-source differences.
forbidden_wording: Directly comparable clinical reliability estimates across model families.

claim_text: Open code, logs, and configs guarantee reproducibility.
evidence: Open artifacts improve auditability, but independent reproduction on another environment is still needed.
required_wording: Say open artifacts improve auditability and support future independent reproduction.
forbidden_wording: Guaranteed reproducibility.

claim_text: Apple Silicon results generalize to other hardware.
evidence: Hardware and MPS behavior are part of the low-resource stress-test setting.
required_wording: Treat Apple Silicon as the studied consumer-hardware setting.
forbidden_wording: Hardware-generalizable performance claim.

claim_text: Using public datasets removes all ethical or governance considerations.
evidence: Public data lowers privacy risk, but dataset artifacts, label ambiguity, language-editing declaration, and claim safety still require governance.
required_wording: Say public data lowers privacy risk but does not remove claim-safety obligations.
forbidden_wording: No ethics or governance issues because all data are public.
